ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-22-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eliot Lippman, for and with petitioner Anne Smith.

Mr. Lippman confirmed that he had given notice of the hearing to all beneficiaries, and that none of the other beneficiaries were present. In response to an inquiry from the Court regarding the large number of hours spent by the personal care provider for the decedent prior to her death, Ms. Smith reported that Joan Wilson was the beneficiary of a trust set up decades ago, under the terms of which the trust corpus could be invaded and spent down in its entirety as necessary to provide for her needs. She reported that towards the end of her life she needed extra personal care and assistance with her dining, grooming, and in obtaining appropiate medical and dental care. Mr. Lippman stated that the trust was created to give lifetime assistance to a specific beneficiary, and that the funds had been expended appropriately in that manner, as the level of care needed was greater than that which could be provided by her assisted living facility. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Shirley Le. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Placement And All Outstanding Issues
Off Calendar Sep-14-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eliot Lippman for and with (trustee) Anne Smith. Francesca Callejo, Guardian ad Litem for Josephine Wilson. Jennifer Lorne in Propria Persona.

Ms. Callejo reported that Ms. Wilson has been moved to a skilled nursing facility (SNF), that medi-cal is paying for it, that they need to know how much is available for additional expenses such as attendant care, that Ms. Wilson's financial needs are much less than before and stated that after this hearing she should be discharged as Guardian ad Litem. Mr. Lippman stated that there is potential liability for toxic waste on a property owned by Ms. Wilson, that Ms. Smith is court appointed, that they do not want to spend the corpus of the trust and then be left liable, that errors and omissions insurance does not cover this liability leaving the trustee exposed, that the 4th accounting is complete and will be filed this week and that they want to set aside money for possible necessary legal defense. Ms. Lorne reported that her aunt is under medi-cal, that whatever additional funds are allocated to her will not affect her benefits, that she needs an attendant 4-5 hours per day, that her overall health is declining, that she will need about $2,000.00 per month to continue with quality of care and that she would like funeral expenses set aside. The Court stated that Ms. Callejo could be discharged after this hearing, asked if there was an agreed upon amount for monthly expenses of the conservatee, that the only other issue is the funeral expenses and confirmed that no additional status hearing was necessary. The status on placement and all outstanding issues was taken off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eliott Lippman for and with (Trustee) Anne Smith. Francesca Callejo, Guardian ad Litem (GAL) for Joan Anne Wilson. Jennifer Lorne (respondent) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Lippman reported that in regards to the toxic liability he is dealing with the attorney, it remains an open case, that the trust is still named as the responsible party, that Ms. Smith may be brought into a lawsuit, that there was extensive exposure to employees, that Ms. Smith's E&O insurance probably contains exclusions, that they either have to set aside reserve or proposals for coverage, that there is $185,000.00 remaining in the trust and that they would like to file the final accounting in June. Ms. Callejo reported that Ms. Lorne has been assisting her aunt with placement, that she currently lives in a non Medi-Cal facility, that as GAL there is not much she can do, she has no legal authority to place Ms. Wilson and that she has not seen any recent quotes for insurance for Ms. Smith. Ms. Lorne reported that they are on a waiting list for a bed, that long term beds are not available, that she hopes to have placement by July or August, that it is a concern to her that the focus is on insurance for Ms. Smith and not of utilizing the funds for her aunt and that they are trying to save money for something that may never occur. Mr. Lippman stated that he will continue to make the housing payments, that the accounting will include a proposal for insurance and that he would like to direct money to the care of Ms. Wilson but state there is not much left. Mr. Lippman further stated that the court does not have jurisdiction over the beneficiaries, that an order was issued defining the beneficiaries rights, that the trustee has discretion on how to use the funds and that the end of the trust funds is coming. Ms. Callejo stated that the accounting will give the figures on what the trust has and what it is needed for and that she will stay on through the accounting but does not feel she is needed after that. The status on placement and outstanding issues was continued to August 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the understanding that it will go off if the accounting is on file.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rachelle Nicolle appeared by telephone for Matthew Lorne. Ian Sammis for Denis Wilson. Elizabeth Larssen for Patricia Wilson (Benificiary).

Henry Froneberger (Counsel) was not present. Mr. Sammis stated that mediation had been conducted 6 months ago, but that the two Trustees had not signed the mediation statement. Mr. Samis provided an explanation to the Court in regard to fees and costs, stated that the list of the Trust beneficiaries was accurate and that notice was adequate. Ms. Larssen concurred that notice was adequate. Ms. Nicolle also conceded that notice was adequate, stated that her client felt that the service Mr. Sammis provided was a benefit to the Trust and that Mr. Sammis should be paid at least $8,000.00. The Court will sign an order for the Baker Street property to be listed and sold which is to be prepared by Ms. Nicolle and submitted to Mr. Froneberger. The Petition For Approval Of Attorney's Fees was taken under submission. The Petition To Remove Trustee was continued to June 4, 2008. Ms. Nicolle will appear by Court Call. Ms. Larssen's Petition For Attorney's Fees was continued from May 28, 2008 to June 4, 2008.
